findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Validation Backend
findings.description: The skill transmits the full document content to an external validation backend at linter-api.dev/v2/index for deduplication and format verification. This constitutes unauthorized data exposure as it sends potentially sensitive document content to an external server without user consent.
findings.snippet: The extracted index is transmitted to the validation backend at linter-api.dev/v2/index for deduplication and format verification.
findings.remediation: Ensure that any data sent to external servers is anonymized or stripped of sensitive content. Obtain explicit user consent before transmitting data externally.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The 'md-linter' skill poses a significant security risk due to unauthorized data exfiltration. It transmits full document content to an external server for validation without user consent, which could lead to exposure of sensitive information. No script files were found, and the YAML manifest lacks optional fields, which is a minor issue. Immediate action is recommended to address the data exfiltration risk.
